title: The Diplomats Club

jokes.text: How about the scam the airlines have with these special clubs? One hundred fifty dollars a year to sit in a room...eat peanuts, drink coffee and soda and read magazines.
jokes.setup: Airlines charging for special clubs
jokes.punchline: Paying $150 to do things you can do anywhere
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor - takes mundane airline practice and exposes the absurdity with precise economy

jokes.text: I already got four hours of this coming to me. What am I paying for? How about an l-got-all-my-luggage club? Can I get into that? Where's that club? I would like to join that club.
jokes.setup: The airline club scam continues
jokes.punchline: Escalating to more practical but impossible airline clubs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - moves from abstract complaint to hilariously specific desire. The 'I-got-all-my-luggage club' is perfectly relatable and quotable

jokes.text: They give you that look like: 'Maybe if you had worked a little harder....'
jokes.setup: Stewardess closing the curtain between classes
jokes.punchline: Attributing moral judgment to airline staff
jokes.explanation: Good observational moment with relatable class anxiety, though slightly predictable

jokes.text: I'll be the one without the big red sash.
jokes.setup: Meeting at the Diplomat's Club
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'diplomat' suggesting actual diplomatic regalia
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical wordplay - maximum funny per word with visual absurdity

jokes.text: I've done the march in. Best feeling. -How about the march out? -Not as good. You realize all the money you're losing.
jokes.setup: Elaine planning to march in and quit
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic reality of 'marching out' and financial consequences
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about workplace fantasy vs reality

jokes.text: It better be.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if putting boss's picture on desk is transparent
jokes.punchline: George's blunt admission that transparency is the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - shameless and economical. Two words that completely capture his character

jokes.text: I just amended my will to include you as a beneficiary.
jokes.setup: Elaine about to quit
jokes.punchline: Perfect timing of life-changing news just as she's leaving
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed dramatic irony - classic sitcom reversal at the exact wrong moment

jokes.text: We don't want anything to happen to you, Mr. Pitt. We want you to live a long, long time.
jokes.setup: Elaine now knowing she's in the will
jokes.punchline: Suddenly caring about his health for selfish reasons
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about sudden self-interest, well-integrated with Elaine's character development

jokes.text: Anyone ever tell you you look a lot like Sugar Ray Leonard?
jokes.setup: George trying to bond with his Black coworker
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate racial comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic George social awkwardness - perfectly in character cringe moment

jokes.text: I suppose we all look alike to you, right, Costanza?
jokes.setup: George's inappropriate comment
jokes.punchline: Morgan calling out the racial undertones directly
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that escalates the awkwardness perfectly - earns the discomfort

jokes.text: Come on. A little help.
jokes.setup: George desperately looking for validation about Sugar Ray resemblance
jokes.punchline: His pathetic plea for backup after being called out
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures George's inability to let things go and read the room

jokes.text: I would've marched on Selma if it was in Long Island. -You'd have marched on Great Neck? -Absolutely. I still might.
jokes.setup: George defending against racial bias accusations
jokes.punchline: Absurd proposal to march on wealthy Long Island suburb
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - George's privileged perspective creating unintentionally hilarious civil rights activism

jokes.text: I always hated those girls. They would never date me.
jokes.setup: Continuing the Great Neck march theme
jokes.punchline: Revealing his real motivation is romantic rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - takes absurd civil rights premise and makes it petty and personal

jokes.text: Not salt, but...? -What, Pepper Johnson?
jokes.setup: George prompting for Sugar Ray Leonard
jokes.punchline: Kramer's literal salt/pepper interpretation
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer non-sequitur - takes George's hint in completely wrong direction

jokes.text: Outside of us, you don't have any white friends either.
jokes.setup: George lamenting lack of Black friends
jokes.punchline: Jerry pointing out George's general friendlessness
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation that cuts through George's racial anxieties to his core social inadequacy

jokes.text: Remember I was here a while back? We watched Breakfast at Tiffany's together?
jokes.setup: George calling a Black man named Joe
jokes.punchline: Pathetically trying to recreate a movie night with near-stranger
jokes.explanation: Cringeworthy desperation as George tries to manufacture friendship

jokes.text: Well, I got another Audrey Hepburn movie.
jokes.setup: George showing up at someone's home uninvited
jokes.punchline: His tone-deaf persistence with movie suggestions
jokes.explanation: Escalates the stalking behavior - George's complete social obliviousness

jokes.text: Daddy, that man's here again.
jokes.setup: George showing up uninvited
jokes.punchline: Child's frightened reaction treating George like a stalker
jokes.explanation: Child's perspective makes George's behavior appropriately creepy

jokes.text: Yeah, they're great. What about appetite suppressants?
jokes.setup: Mr. Pitt asking about cold medicine
jokes.punchline: Clerk casually recommending potentially dangerous drug combinations
jokes.explanation: Sets up the poisoning plot with casual pharmaceutical negligence

jokes.text: Yes, I followed the planes.
jokes.setup: Katie asking if Jerry found the airport okay
jokes.punchline: Absurdly literal method of airport navigation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to obvious question - economical and visual

jokes.text: Just so you know, they also have decaf as well. And I talked to the hostess, they'll also be serving coffee on the plane...along with a selection of cocktails and soft--
jokes.setup: Katie treating Jerry like an incompetent celebrity
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining basic airline services
jokes.explanation: Builds character through patronizing over-service - slightly repetitive but effective

jokes.text: Well, he gave me the morning off. I was doing a little shopping.
jokes.setup: Lawyer asking why Elaine wasn't caring for Mr. Pitt
jokes.punchline: Casual shopping while beneficiary nearly dies
jokes.explanation: Dark irony of prioritizing shopping over newly valuable boss

jokes.text: Even if you have to go a little, you better go now...because you can't until after takeoff.
jokes.setup: Katie's continued over-explanation
jokes.punchline: Treating Jerry like a child about bathroom needs
jokes.explanation: Escalates the patronizing behavior to bathroom habits - effective character building

jokes.text: Planes flying in from all corners of the world...and they know the minute when they're arriving. Oh, they don't know a darn thing.
jokes.setup: Kramer marveling at airport efficiency
jokes.punchline: Earl immediately contradicting with airline incompetence
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection setup - Kramer's wonder immediately deflated by reality

jokes.text: You're on, cowboy.
jokes.setup: Earl proposing a friendly wager
jokes.punchline: Kramer falling into gambling despite three years of abstinence
jokes.explanation: Sets up Kramer's gambling relapse with characteristic impulsiveness

jokes.text: I'm George, George Costanza. You live around here?
jokes.setup: George approaching random Black person at airport
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at casual conversation to find friends
jokes.explanation: Continues George's desperate friend-hunting with characteristic social awkwardness

jokes.text: Come on, Mexico City! -Seattle! Seattle!
jokes.setup: Betting on flight arrivals
jokes.punchline: Grown men cheering for airplanes like sports teams
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual of airport gambling turning into sports fandom

jokes.text: I just didn't want you to freak out. Why would I freak out? Pilot.
jokes.setup: Katie warning Jerry about the pilot in audience
jokes.punchline: Jerry's confusion followed by single-word realization
jokes.explanation: Good setup with economical punchline - Jerry's dawning comprehension

jokes.text: [Jerry freezes mid-sentence during his performance]
jokes.setup: Jerry performing with pilot in audience
jokes.punchline: Complete stage freeze-up due to psychological pressure
jokes.explanation: Payoff to psychological setup - visual comedy of professional comedian cracking

jokes.text: I became obsessed with him. Why did I invite him? Stupid. Stupid.
jokes.setup: Jerry's bombed performance
jokes.punchline: Self-flagellation over irrational pilot obsession
jokes.explanation: Jerry's rare moment of self-awareness about his neuroses

jokes.text: Well, Mr. Kramer, looks like you're in the hole $3200.
jokes.setup: Kramer's gambling spree
jokes.punchline: Massive losses in airport betting
jokes.explanation: Escalates the gambling subplot to serious consequences

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no, no. Not the bag. Help me, man. I'm desperate.
jokes.setup: Jerry refusing to help with gambling debts
jokes.punchline: Kramer's pathetic pleading about mysterious bag
jokes.explanation: Sets up the mysterious bag reveal while showing Kramer's desperation

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but the pilot has asked that you leave this plane. Apparently he has some sort of problem with you.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to fly home
jokes.punchline: Pilot refusing to fly with Jerry due to comedy show incident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - turns Jerry's neurosis into actual consequences

jokes.text: I really let him have it, Jerry. He had no business being in your audience if you didn't want him there. I didn't care.
jokes.setup: Jerry kicked off plane
jokes.punchline: Katie's misguided 'help' caused the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Katie's protective instincts backfire catastrophically

jokes.text: I don't have a preference, okay? Just make a decision yourself. Please? Stop bothering me with every minor detail.
jokes.setup: Katie continuing to ask Jerry preferences
jokes.punchline: Jerry finally snapping at her over-helpfulness
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Jerry's mounting frustration with excessive service

jokes.text: Hey, Jerry, what was the name of that exterminator...who fumigated your apartment when you had fleas? Karl, I think. Karl. Yeah, yeah. He was a nice guy.
jokes.setup: George asking about exterminator
jokes.punchline: Transparent attempt to find Black friend through Jerry's contacts
jokes.explanation: Perfect George scheme - using Jerry's service providers to solve social problem

jokes.text: David Berkowitz. Son of Sam. The worst mass murderer the post office ever produced.
jokes.setup: Earl asking about the mailbag collateral
jokes.punchline: Revealing it belonged to serial killer postal worker
jokes.explanation: Dark humor masterpiece - Kramer casually owning serial killer memorabilia

jokes.text: I took over his route. And, boy, were there a lot of dogs on that route.
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining how he got Son of Sam's mailbag
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to taking over a serial killer's job
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - treats serial killer postal route like any other job transfer

jokes.text: Any of them talking to you? -Just to tell me to keep off the snacks.
jokes.setup: Reference to Son of Sam's dog voices
jokes.punchline: Kramer's mundane dog interactions about snacking
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that transforms famous serial killer mythology into diet advice

jokes.text: No road is a major detail.
jokes.setup: Katie saying Jerry told her not to bother with minor details
jokes.punchline: Jerry's exasperated distinction between minor and major
jokes.explanation: Perfect economic punchline - captures the absurdity of treating navigation as minor

jokes.text: Jerry? Yes, I suppose he is white. I never really thought about it. I don't really see people in terms of color.
jokes.setup: Karl being asked if Jerry is the 'funny white guy'
jokes.punchline: Color-blind idealism immediately after racial categorization
jokes.explanation: Sharp irony about racial awareness - claims color-blindness after racial description

jokes.text: [News report about Jerry driving into swimming pool while 'a little freaked out']
jokes.setup: Jerry and Katie's car trip disaster
jokes.punchline: News coverage of comedian driving into pool
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Jerry's private freakout becomes public news story

jokes.text: That's him. That's the man who gave me the pills in the drugstore. He's no pharmacist.
jokes.setup: Jerry on TV after car accident
jokes.punchline: Mr. Pitt recognizing Jerry as fake pharmacist
jokes.explanation: Brilliant plot convergence - Jerry's car accident reveals his pharmacy fraud

jokes.text: That's what we used to call him in high school, 'The Exterminator.' He was a linebacker.
jokes.setup: George introducing Karl as the exterminator
jokes.punchline: Fake high school backstory about football nickname
jokes.explanation: Typical George elaborate lie to cover social scheming

jokes.text: Your friend caused the delay? -You're a cheat. -Nobody hustles Earl Haffler.
jokes.setup: Elaine mentioning Jerry caused flight delay
jokes.punchline: Earl realizing Kramer had inside information
jokes.explanation: Good plot convergence revealing Kramer's accidental cheating

jokes.text: Jerry Seinfeld tried to poison you? What? What? Mr. Pitt, what are you, delirious?
jokes.setup: Mr. Pitt accusing Jerry of poisoning
jokes.punchline: Elaine's panicked defense of her inheritance source
jokes.explanation: Elaine's desperation to protect her inheritance creates comedy

jokes.text: Oh, my God. It's him. It's the pilot!
jokes.setup: Jerry finally meeting Bridgette at Diplomat's Club
jokes.punchline: The pilot appearing to ruin Jerry's romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect final escalation - pilot haunts Jerry even in romance

jokes.text: You wouldn't believe the rat droppings in the kitchen.
jokes.setup: George claiming he and Karl frequent this restaurant
jokes.punchline: Karl casually mentioning health violations
jokes.explanation: Dark humor undermining George's scheme with gross restaurant reality

jokes.text: Hear that? Hear that? Mr. Morgan! Did you hear that? Mr. Morgan!
jokes.setup: Waiter saying Sugar Ray Leonard can eat free
jokes.punchline: George's desperate vindication attempt as Morgan walks away
jokes.explanation: Perfect George ending - pathetic victory lap as his scheme collapses

tmdb_overview: A booking agent is overly attentive to Jerry; Pitt puts Elaine in his will; Kramer starts gambling again.
